title: Design File System
problem_statement: Design a file system that allows creating new file paths with values and retrieving values by path. createPath should succeed only if the parent path exists and the path does not already exist.

examples.input: createPath("/a", 1) -> true; get("/a") -> 1; createPath("/a/b", 2) -> true; get("/a/b") -> 2
examples.explanation: Paths are only created when their parent path exists and stored values can be retrieved by exact path.

constraints: Paths consist of "/" and lowercase English letters
At most 10^4 calls to createPath and get
intuition: Store each valid path in a map and validate parent existence by checking the prefix before insertion.

approaches.name: HashMap with Parent Validation
approaches.description: Map every created path to its value and ensure a new path is only inserted when its immediate parent path already exists.
approaches.why_not: A trie is possible, but a hash map is simpler and efficient for exact path lookups.

interview_qa.q: How do you ensure a new path is valid?
interview_qa.a: Verify the path does not already exist and that its parent path is present in the map before inserting it.

interview_qa.q: Why use a map instead of a tree?
interview_qa.a: A hash map provides direct path lookups with simple parent checks and is fast enough for exact path queries.
